steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is the subject of "adapted" and must be a noun phrase. The plural pronoun "their" later in the sentence must agree with whatever fills the blank.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'island's residents': Singular possessive "island's" modifies plural "residents," which agrees with "their." ✓
  • Choice B — 'islands resident's': "islands" has no apostrophe, so it cannot function as a possessive modifier; singular "resident's" also clashes with the plural pronoun "their." ✗
  • Choice C — 'island's resident's': Double possessive creates an ungrammatical noun phrase as the subject. ✗
  • Choice D — 'islands residents'': "islands" has no apostrophe and cannot function as a possessive modifier; without that relationship the noun phrase is structurally broken. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
